source.source_meaning_cn: 刻 画； 描写

headline.core_en: to describe, outline, or mark the boundaries of something clearly
to describe, outline, or mark the boundaries of something clearly

familiar_bridge.summary: line 可以帮你记：delineate 像用线把轮廓画出来，也可以把观点边界讲清。

core.main: delineate 既可指描绘轮廓，也可指清楚说明概念、职责或区别的边界。

usage_groups.group_title: 勾勒轮廓或清楚说明边界
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于描写形状、特征、职责、区别或论点结构。

examples.sentence: The map delineates the boundaries of the protected area.
examples.translation: 这张地图标出了保护区的边界。
examples.teaching_note: delineate the boundaries 是具体的“划清边界”。

examples.sentence: The author carefully delineates the difference between influence and control.
examples.translation: 作者仔细说明了影响和控制之间的区别。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象语境中表示把区别讲清楚。

examples.sentence: The map delineates the boundaries of the protected area.
examples.translation: 这张地图标出了保护区的边界。
examples.teaching_note: delineate the boundaries 是具体的“划清边界”。

examples.sentence: The author carefully delineates the difference between influence and control.
examples.translation: 作者仔细说明了影响和控制之间的区别。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象语境中表示把区别讲清楚。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness described the scene in careful detail.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: describe 宽泛；delineate 更强调清楚勾勒结构或边界。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The professor outlined the argument before discussing details.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: outline 强调给出框架；delineate 更强调边界和细节清晰。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The treaty demarcated the border between the two states.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: demarcate 更偏实际划界；delineate 可具体也可抽象。

antonyms.example_sentence: The jargon obscured the writer’s main point.
antonyms.difference_note: obscure 让内容变不清楚，与 delineate 的讲清楚相反。

extension.root_affix: line / linea 与“线”有关；delineate 可理解为“用线画出轮廓”。

word_family.example_sentence: The delineation of responsibilities reduced confusion.
The line marks the edge of the property.

related_words.example_sentence: The outline shows the structure of the essay.

source.source_meaning_cn: 禁 运； 贸易 禁令

headline.core_en: an official ban on trade or movement, or to impose such a ban
an official ban on trade or movement, or to impose such a ban

familiar_bridge.summary: ban 是普通的“禁止”；embargo 多指政府或官方对贸易、货物、信息发布等施加禁令。

core.main: embargo 的重点是正式、官方的限制，尤其常见于 trade embargo / impose an embargo / lift an embargo。

usage_groups.group_title: 官方禁运或贸易限制
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于国家、政府或机构禁止货物、贸易或信息流通。

examples.sentence: The government imposed an embargo on weapons sales.
examples.translation: 政府对武器销售实施了禁运。
examples.teaching_note: impose an embargo 是“实施禁运”。

examples.sentence: The agreement lifted the embargo and allowed food shipments to resume.
examples.translation: 协议解除了禁运，使食品运输得以恢复。
examples.teaching_note: lift an embargo 是“解除禁令”。

examples.sentence: The government imposed an embargo on weapons sales.
examples.translation: 政府对武器销售实施了禁运。
examples.teaching_note: impose an embargo 是“实施禁运”。

examples.sentence: The agreement lifted the embargo and allowed food shipments to resume.
examples.translation: 协议解除了禁运，使食品运输得以恢复。
examples.teaching_note: lift an embargo 是“解除禁令”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The city announced a ban on single-use plastic bags.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: ban 最普通；embargo 更正式，常指贸易、货物或信息发布限制。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: New restrictions reduced the flow of goods across the border.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: restriction 范围更广；embargo 是更强、更正式的禁令。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The blockade prevented supplies from entering the port.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: blockade 常是军事或物理封锁；embargo 多是法律或政策禁令。

antonyms.example_sentence: The permit gave the company permission to export the goods.
antonyms.difference_note: permission 表示被允许，和 embargo 的禁止方向相反。

extension.root_affix: 重点记官方禁令/贸易限制这一语境。

word_family.meaning: 被禁运的；被禁止发布的
word_family.example_sentence: The embargoed goods remained at the port.

related_words.example_sentence: The blockade cut off supplies.
The ban affected several exports.

source.source_note: From archived Unit 2 OCR extraction; OCR text remains internal evidence only.
source.source_meaning_cn: 计策； 手段

headline.core_en: a clever plan, often used to gain an advantage
a clever plan, often used to gain an advantage

familiar_bridge.summary: play 是“玩”；ploy 像是为了达到目的而设计的小策略，常带一点不完全坦率的感觉。

core.main: ploy 是有目的的策略或手段，重点在“设计出来让别人接受、分心或上钩”。

usage_groups.group_title: 为获得优势而设计的策略
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调计划带有目的性，有时显得不完全真诚。

examples.sentence: The discount was a marketing ploy to attract first-time buyers.
examples.translation: 这个折扣是一种吸引首次购买者的营销手段。
examples.teaching_note: marketing ploy 常指商业策略。

examples.sentence: Critics called the speech a political ploy rather than a sincere proposal.
examples.translation: 批评者认为这场演讲是政治手段，而不是真诚的提议。
examples.teaching_note: political ploy 常带负面评价。

examples.sentence: The discount was a marketing ploy to attract first-time buyers.
examples.translation: 这个折扣是一种吸引首次购买者的营销手段。
examples.teaching_note: marketing ploy 常指商业策略。

examples.sentence: Critics called the speech a political ploy rather than a sincere proposal.
examples.translation: 批评者认为这场演讲是政治手段，而不是真诚的提议。
examples.teaching_note: political ploy 常带负面评价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The trick persuaded customers to buy more than they needed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trick 更像欺骗性花招；ploy 可以更策略化。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The strategy helped the group win support.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: strategy 中性；ploy 常暗示为了优势而设计的手段。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The spy used a ruse to enter the building.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ruse 更强调欺骗性。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her sincere proposal addressed the problem directly.
antonyms.difference_note: sincere proposal 与 ploy 的策略性、可能不真诚形成对照。

extension.root_affix: ploy ，重点记它常带“策略性手段”的语气。

related_words.example_sentence: The offer was a ploy to win attention.
The ruse fooled the guards.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: ploy 已在 10 词批次中完成逐项深审；新内容避免旧模板句，关系和派生均围绕本词真实语义。 usage_tree 已按语义场逐项审查。
issues_found: 旧稿容易把 ploy 只写成普通“手段”，没有呈现策略性和可能的负面语气。

headline.core_en: to stop, catch, or receive something before it reaches its destination
to stop, catch, or receive something before it reaches its destination

familiar_bridge.summary: inter- 是“在中间”；intercept 就是在东西到达目的地之前从中间截住。

core.main: intercept 强调在途中把信息、球、车辆或人截住。

usage_groups.group_title: 在途中截住信息、物品或行动
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某物本来要到达某处，但中途被截住。

examples.sentence: Officials intercepted the message before it reached the rebel group.
examples.translation: 官员在消息传到叛军组织之前将其截获。
examples.teaching_note: intercept a message 是信息被途中截住。

examples.sentence: The defender intercepted the pass and changed the course of the game.
examples.translation: 防守队员截住传球，改变了比赛走势。
examples.teaching_note: 体育语境中常见 intercept a pass。

examples.sentence: Officials intercepted the message before it reached the rebel group.

source.source_meaning_cn: 相反 地； 反 过 来

headline.core_en: used to introduce the opposite or reverse side of a statement
used to introduce the opposite or reverse side of a statement

familiar_bridge.summary: converse 有“反过来”的意思；conversely 用来把上一句话倒过来看。

core.main: conversely 是连接词，帮助作者从一个方向转到相反方向或互补方向。

usage_groups.group_title: 在论证中引出相反方向
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于把作者的论证转向对照或反向关系。
common_patterns: A may affect B; conversely, B may affect A

examples.sentence: Some reforms reduce costs; conversely, others require heavy investment.
examples.translation: 有些改革会降低成本；相反，另一些改革需要大量投入。
examples.teaching_note: conversely 引出相反情况。

examples.sentence: The environment shapes behavior; conversely, behavior can reshape the environment.
examples.translation: 环境塑造行为；反过来，行为也能重塑环境。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于说明双向关系。

examples.sentence: Some reforms reduce costs; conversely, others require heavy investment.
examples.translation: 有些改革会降低成本；相反，另一些改革需要大量投入。
examples.teaching_note: conversely 引出相反情况。

examples.sentence: The environment shapes behavior; conversely, behavior can reshape the environment.
examples.translation: 环境塑造行为；反过来，行为也能重塑环境。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于说明双向关系。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan is costly; on the other hand, it may save time.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: on the other hand 更口语/常用；conversely 更简洁正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Urban areas grew rapidly; rural areas, in contrast, declined.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: in contrast 强调对比；conversely 常指反向关系。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: By contrast, the second experiment produced stable results.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: by contrast 更强调两者差异。

antonyms.example_sentence: Similarly, the second group showed improvement.
antonyms.difference_note: similarly 引出相同方向；conversely 引出相反方向。

extension.root_affix: converse 可指“相反命题/反向关系”；conversely 是连接副词。

word_family.example_sentence: The converse of the claim is not always true.
Their conversation lasted for hours.

related_words.example_sentence: The variables have an inverse relationship.

headline.core_en: a secret and often dishonest plan to gain power or advantage
a secret and often dishonest plan to gain power or advantage

familiar_bridge.summary: machine 让人想到机制；machination 像暗处运转的一套阴谋机器。

core.main: machination 多用复数 machinations，指为权力或利益暗中策划的复杂阴谋。

usage_groups.group_title: 暗中运作的复杂阴谋
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 多用于政治、权力斗争或组织内部暗斗。

examples.sentence: The minister survived years of political machinations.
examples.translation: 这位部长挺过了多年的政治阴谋。
examples.teaching_note: political machinations 指政治斗争中的暗中策划。

examples.sentence: The novel exposes the machinations by which the family preserves its power.
examples.translation: 小说揭露了这个家族维持权力的种种阴谋。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于政治、家族、组织权力斗争。

examples.sentence: The minister survived years of political machinations.

examples.sentence: The novel exposes the machinations by which the family preserves its power.

examples.teaching_note: 常用于政治、家族、组织权力斗争。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plot to remove the leader failed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: plot 更普通；machination 更强调复杂、暗中运作。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The scheme was designed to mislead investors.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: scheme 可中性或负面；machination 通常负面。

source.source_meaning_cn: 功效； 效力

headline.core_en: the ability of a method, treatment, or policy to produce the intended result
the ability of a method, treatment, or policy to produce the intended result

familiar_bridge.summary: effect 是“效果”；efficacy 更正式，强调某方法是否真的能达到预期效果。

core.main: efficacy 常用于药物、政策、方法、治疗、制度是否有效。

usage_groups.group_title: 方法、药物或政策是否真的有效
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 关注某方案能否产生预期结果。
common_patterns: test the efficacy of a method
question the efficacy of a policy

examples.sentence: The trial tested the efficacy of the new vaccine.
examples.translation: 这项试验检验了新疫苗的功效。
examples.teaching_note: 医学语境中非常常见。

examples.sentence: Critics questioned the efficacy of the policy in reducing inequality.
examples.translation: 批评者质疑这项政策在减少不平等方面的效果。
examples.teaching_note: 政策和方法也可谈 efficacy。

examples.sentence: The trial tested the efficacy of the new vaccine.

examples.sentence: Critics questioned the efficacy of the policy in reducing inequality.

examples.teaching_note: 政策和方法也可谈 efficacy。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The effectiveness of the program was measured over five years.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: effectiveness 更普通；efficacy 更正式，常用于药物、政策、方法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The usefulness of the tool depends on the task.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: usefulness 更宽泛；efficacy 强调达成预期结果的能力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The potency of the medicine surprised researchers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: potency 常指药力或影响强度；efficacy 强调是否有效。

antonyms.example_sentence: The inefficacy of the treatment became clear.
antonyms.difference_note: inefficacy 是 efficacy 的直接反义。

extension.root_affix: efficacy 和 efficacious 同族，都和“产生效果”有关。

word_family.example_sentence: The therapy proved efficacious in early trials.

related_words.example_sentence: The new rule was effective immediately.

headline.core_en: able to produce the intended result

headline.core_en: to make an idea, law, or belief widely known or officially announced

familiar_bridge.summary: promote 是推动；promulgate 更正式，像把法律、思想或教义公开发布并传播出去。

core.main: promulgate 常用于法律、政策、思想、信条的正式公布或广泛传播。

usage_groups.group_title: 正式公布或传播法律、政策、思想
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把法律、政策或思想正式公布，或有目的地传播出去。

examples.sentence: The government promulgated new rules on data privacy.

headline.core_en: a sharp increase, or to rise sharply
a sharp increase, or to rise sharply

familiar_bridge.summary: spike 本来是“尖刺”；图表上突然冒出的尖峰，就是 spike。

core.main: spike 常指价格、病例、需求、犯罪率等突然快速上升。

usage_groups.group_title: 数字、价格或需求突然形成尖峰
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调短时间内快速上升。

examples.sentence: The city saw a sudden spike in housing prices.
examples.translation: 这座城市的房价突然大幅上涨。
examples.teaching_note: a spike in prices 是常见图表/经济表达。

examples.sentence: Demand for masks spiked during the outbreak.

examples.sentence: The city saw a sudden spike in housing prices.

headline.core_en: to irritate or offend someone, or bold impudence
to irritate or offend someone, or bold impudence

familiar_bridge.summary: gall 可以想成“刺痛人的冒犯感”；作名词还可指厚颜无耻。

core.main: gall 作动词时是让人恼火或受辱；作名词时常见 have the gall to do sth，表示竟敢厚着脸皮做某事。

usage_groups.group_title: 冒犯到让人恼火；厚颜无耻
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 兼有动词和名词用法，语气比 annoy 更强。
common_patterns: have the gall to do something

examples.sentence: It galled her that the committee ignored her warnings.
examples.translation: 委员会无视她的警告，这让她十分恼火。

examples.sentence: He had the gall to ask for a raise after missing every deadline.
examples.translation: 他每个截止日期都错过了，竟然还厚着脸皮要求加薪。
examples.teaching_note: have the gall to do sth 表示“竟敢厚颜无耻地做”。

examples.sentence: It galled her that the committee ignored her warnings.
examples.translation: 委员会无视她的警告，这让她十分恼火。

examples.sentence: He had the gall to ask for a raise after missing every deadline.
examples.translation: 他每个截止日期都错过了，竟然还厚着脸皮要求加薪。
examples.teaching_note: have the gall to do sth 表示“竟敢厚颜无耻地做”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The constant noise annoyed the residents.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: annoy 普通；gall 更强，带冒犯、屈辱感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His careless tone irritated the audience.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: irritate 可指轻微烦躁；gall 更像被冒犯到。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The insult continued to rankle for years.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: rankle 强调怨气持续；gall 强调当下冒犯感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The apology pleased the audience.
antonyms.difference_note: please 与 gall 的使人恼火相反。

extension.root_affix: gall 可作动词“使恼火”，也可作名词“厚颜无耻”。galling 是它的形容词。

word_family.meaning: 令人恼火的；令人屈辱的
word_family.example_sentence: The defeat was especially galling.

related_words.example_sentence: The restriction began to chafe.

headline.core_cn: 令人恼火的；令人屈辱的
headline.core_en: very annoying or humiliating, especially because something feels unfair

headline.core_cn: 令人恼火的；令人屈辱的
headline.core_en: very annoying or humiliating, especially because something feels unfair

familiar_bridge.summary: gall 是“使恼火/冒犯”；galling 就是让人觉得被冒犯、被羞辱的。

core.main: galling 常形容失败、不公、批评或对比让人特别难受。

usage_groups.group_title: 不公平或屈辱感带来的恼火
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 比 annoying 更强，常含不甘或被羞辱。

examples.sentence: The loss was galling because the team had led for most of the game.
examples.translation: 这场失利格外令人恼火，因为球队大部分时间都处于领先。
examples.teaching_note: galling defeat 常带屈辱感。

examples.sentence: It was galling to see less qualified candidates receive the award.
examples.translation: 看到资历较浅的候选人获奖让人很不舒服。
examples.teaching_note: It is galling to... 表示某事令人窝火。

examples.sentence: The loss was galling because the team had led for most of the game.

examples.teaching_note: It is galling to... 表示某事令人窝火。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The delay was annoying but manageable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: annoying 普通；galling 更强，常带不公平或屈辱感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The repeated interruptions were irritating.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: irritating 偏烦躁；galling 偏被冒犯或不甘。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The inconsistency in the data was vexing.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vexing 较正式，强调令人困扰；galling 更有屈辱/愤懑。

antonyms.example_sentence: The result was gratifying after months of effort.

extension.root_affix: galling 来自 gall；学习时成组记 gall / galling.

headline.core_en: a series of things that happen one after another, often quickly
a series of things that happen one after another, often quickly

familiar_bridge.summary: cascade 本来是瀑布一样层层落下；抽象用法里就是事情一个接一个连锁发生。

core.main: cascade 强调连续、连锁、层层推进的变化或事件。

usage_groups.group_title: 一连串连锁发生的事件
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 一个变化触发后续变化，像水流层层落下。

examples.sentence: One small error triggered a cascade of problems in the experiment.
examples.translation: 一个小错误在实验中引发了一连串问题。
examples.teaching_note: a cascade of problems 强调问题接连出现。

examples.sentence: Changes in one species can cascade through the entire ecosystem.
examples.translation: 一个物种的变化可能在整个生态系统中层层传导。
examples.teaching_note: 动词 cascade 表示影响继续向下或向外扩散。

examples.sentence: One small error triggered a cascade of problems in the experiment.
examples.translation: 一个小错误在实验中引发了一连串问题。
examples.teaching_note: a cascade of problems 强调问题接连出现。

examples.sentence: Changes in one species can cascade through the entire ecosystem.
examples.translation: 一个物种的变化可能在整个生态系统中层层传导。
examples.teaching_note: 动词 cascade 表示影响继续向下或向外扩散。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lecture presented a series of examples.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: series 只是排列；cascade 强调连锁、快速展开。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A chain of mistakes led to the failure.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: chain 强调连接关系；cascade 更有“接连涌来”的动态感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A succession of storms damaged the coast.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: succession 更正式，强调先后连续；cascade 更强调触发和扩散。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new rule brought the process to a halt.

extension.root_affix: cascade 的具体画面是“瀑布层层落下”，可迁移到抽象的连锁反应。

core.main: seclusion 可以是主动隐居，也可以是被隔离在公众视线之外。

usage_groups.group_title: 远离人群或公众视线
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调安静、隔绝、隐居或不被外界打扰。

examples.sentence: After the scandal, the writer lived in seclusion for several years.
examples.translation: 丑闻之后，这位作家隐居了数年。

examples.sentence: The retreat offers seclusion from the noise of the city.
examples.translation: 这个静修地让人远离城市喧嚣。
examples.teaching_note: seclusion 可表示主动寻找的安静隔绝。

headline.core_en: despite what has just been said
despite what has just been said

familiar_bridge.summary: none the less 可以理解成“不因此减少”：前面有让步，后面仍成立。

core.main: nonetheless 连接让步和转折，表示虽然有困难、限制或反对，结论仍然成立。

usage_groups.group_title: 让步后仍然成立
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 前一句给出阻碍或相反信息，后一句表示结果仍发生。

examples.sentence: The evidence was incomplete; nonetheless, the pattern was clear.
examples.translation: 证据并不完整；尽管如此，模式仍然很清楚。
examples.teaching_note: nonetheless 常放句首连接转折。

examples.sentence: The proposal is expensive but nonetheless worth considering.
examples.translation: 这个提案成本很高，但仍然值得考虑。
examples.teaching_note: but nonetheless 表示让步后保留判断。

examples.sentence: The evidence was incomplete; nonetheless, the pattern was clear.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The road was difficult, but they still continued.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The method is simple; however, it is not always reliable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: however 表示转折；nonetheless 更强调“尽管如此仍然”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The data are limited; nevertheless, they suggest a trend.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: nevertheless 和 nonetheless 很接近，语气同样正式。

antonyms.example_sentence: The data were strong; therefore, the conclusion was persuasive.
antonyms.difference_note: therefore 表示因果推进；nonetheless 表示让步转折。

extension.root_affix: nonetheless 可拆成 none + the + less：前面的困难并没有让后面的结论减少成立。

source.source_meaning_cn: 显 眼 的； 明显 的； 显著 的

headline.core_en: easy to notice, often because something stands out
easy to notice, often because something stands out

familiar_bridge.summary: see 是“看见”；conspicuous 是“特别容易被看见、很难不注意到”。

core.main: conspicuous 可以形容物体显眼，也可以形容行为、缺席或差异非常明显。

usage_groups.group_title: 明显到很难忽视
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某物或某种现象在背景中突出出来。

examples.sentence: Her bright red coat was conspicuous in the gray crowd.
examples.translation: 她鲜红色的外套在人群的灰色调中格外显眼。
examples.teaching_note: 具体视觉上的显眼。

examples.sentence: The report is conspicuous for its lack of evidence.
examples.translation: 这份报告最明显的问题是缺乏证据。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象语境中也可说某个缺陷很突出。

examples.sentence: Her bright red coat was conspicuous in the gray crowd.
examples.translation: 她鲜红色的外套在人群的灰色调中格外显眼。
examples.teaching_note: 具体视觉上的显眼。

examples.sentence: The report is conspicuous for its lack of evidence.
examples.translation: 这份报告最明显的问题是缺乏证据。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象语境中也可说某个缺陷很突出。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The change was noticeable after a week.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: noticeable 普通；conspicuous 更强调突出、难以忽视。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The answer was obvious to everyone.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: obvious 强调容易明白；conspicuous 更强调显眼或突出。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The salient feature of the theory is its simplicity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: salient 更学术，指最突出的特征。

antonyms.example_sentence: The entrance is small and inconspicuous.
antonyms.difference_note: inconspicuous 是直接反义。

extension.root_affix: conspicuous 和 spectacle / inspect 同属“看”的语义场，可用“被看见、很显眼”来记。

word_family.example_sentence: The name was conspicuously absent from the list.
He chose an inconspicuous seat in the back.

related_words.example_sentence: The parade was a spectacle of color.

headline.core_en: completely disordered and hard to control or predict
completely disordered and hard to control or predict

familiar_bridge.summary: chaos 是混乱；chaotic 就是像一团 chaos，缺少秩序和可预测性。

core.main: chaotic 不只是 busy，而是秩序被打乱，情况难以控制或理解。

usage_groups.group_title: 混乱无序、难以控制
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 场面、过程、系统或时期缺少清晰秩序。

examples.sentence: The airport became chaotic after hundreds of flights were canceled.
examples.translation: 数百个航班取消后，机场变得一片混乱。
examples.teaching_note: chaotic scene/situation 指局面失控。

examples.sentence: The essay describes a chaotic transition from empire to republic.
examples.translation: 这篇文章描写了从帝国到共和国的混乱转型。
examples.teaching_note: 历史或政治语境中常修饰 transition。

examples.sentence: The airport became chaotic after hundreds of flights were canceled.

examples.sentence: The essay describes a chaotic transition from empire to republic.
examples.translation: 这篇文章描写了从帝国到共和国的混乱转型。
examples.teaching_note: 历史或政治语境中常修饰 transition。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The room was messy after the party.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: messy 更口语；chaotic 更强调无序、难控制。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The explanation left the students confused.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: confused 可指人困惑；chaotic 多指局面或系统混乱。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The files were arranged in a disordered pile.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disordered 更正式，强调缺少秩序。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evacuation was orderly and calm.
antonyms.difference_note: orderly 与 chaotic 形成直接对照。

extension.root_affix: chaos 是“混乱”；chaotic 是形容词，chaotically 是副词。

word_family.example_sentence: The sudden announcement caused chaos.

headline.core_en: to give up or let go of something, often unwillingly or formally
to give up or let go of something, often unwillingly or formally

familiar_bridge.summary: give up 是普通放弃；relinquish 更正式，常表示让出权力、权利、控制或所有物。

core.main: relinquish 强调主动或被迫放手，把原本掌握的东西交出去。

usage_groups.group_title: 放弃或交出原本掌握的东西
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 权力、控制、权利、职位或物品从自己手里让出。

examples.sentence: The king was forced to relinquish power after the rebellion.
examples.translation: 叛乱之后，国王被迫交出权力。
examples.teaching_note: relinquish power 是高频正式搭配。

examples.sentence: She refused to relinquish her claim to the property.
examples.translation: 她拒绝放弃对这处财产的主张。
examples.teaching_note: relinquish a claim 指放弃权利主张。

examples.sentence: The king was forced to relinquish power after the rebellion.
examples.translation: 叛乱之后，国王被迫交出权力。
examples.teaching_note: relinquish power 是高频正式搭配。

examples.sentence: She refused to relinquish her claim to the property.
examples.translation: 她拒绝放弃对这处财产的主张。
examples.teaching_note: relinquish a claim 指放弃权利主张。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He gave up the search after three days.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: give up 普通；relinquish 更正式，常涉及权力、控制、权利。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The army surrendered the city.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: surrender 常有战败或屈服；relinquish 不一定是投降。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The monarch abdicated the throne.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: abdicate 专指放弃王位或责任；relinquish 更宽。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company retained control of the patents.
antonyms.difference_note: retain 是保留不放，与 relinquish 相反。

extension.root_affix: 重点记正式语境中的 give up / let go of control.

word_family.example_sentence: The relinquishment of control was gradual.
The relinquished territory was later restored.

related_words.example_sentence: He renounced his claim to the title.

source.source_meaning_cn: 反 对； 反击； 反驳

headline.core_en: to respond to something by opposing it or offering an opposite argument
to respond to something by opposing it or offering an opposite argument

familiar_bridge.summary: counter 可以想成“朝相反方向回击”：别人提出观点或行动，你用相反力量回应。

core.main: counter 作动词时强调回应并抵消、反驳或对抗。

usage_groups.group_title: 用相反观点或行动回应
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 对论点、攻击、趋势或影响作出反向回应。

examples.sentence: The scientist countered the criticism with new data.
examples.translation: 科学家用新数据反驳了批评。
examples.teaching_note: counter with evidence 表示用证据反驳。

examples.sentence: The policy was designed to counter the effects of inflation.
examples.translation: 这项政策旨在抵消通货膨胀的影响。
examples.teaching_note: counter the effects of 强调抵消影响。

examples.sentence: The scientist countered the criticism with new data.
examples.translation: 科学家用新数据反驳了批评。
examples.teaching_note: counter with evidence 表示用证据反驳。

examples.sentence: The policy was designed to counter the effects of inflation.
examples.translation: 这项政策旨在抵消通货膨胀的影响。
examples.teaching_note: counter the effects of 强调抵消影响。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Several members opposed the proposal.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: oppose 表示立场反对；counter 更强调回应、反击或抵消。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She answered the objection calmly.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: answer 可普通回应；counter 是带反驳或反向力量的回应。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence refuted the claim.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: refute 强调证明对方错误；counter 可只是提出反论或反制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The data support the author’s argument.
antonyms.difference_note: support 是帮助成立；counter 是反向回应。

extension.root_affix: counter- 作前缀常表示“反向、对抗”，如 counterargument / counterproductive。

word_family.example_sentence: The essay considers a counterargument.
The medicine counteracts the side effects.

headline.core_en: to hold something firmly in place, literally or figuratively
to hold something firmly in place, literally or figuratively

familiar_bridge.summary: anchor 本来是船锚；抽象用法就是把观点、身份或制度固定住。

core.main: anchor 强调提供稳定基础，使某事不漂浮、不松动。

usage_groups.group_title: 提供稳定基础，使某物固定下来
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 可以是物理固定，也可以是思想、身份、制度的根基。

examples.sentence: The boat was anchored near the shore.
examples.translation: 船停锚在岸边附近。
examples.teaching_note: 具体义是用锚固定船。

examples.sentence: The author anchors the argument in historical evidence.
examples.translation: 作者把论证建立在历史证据之上。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象义是让论点有稳固依据。

examples.sentence: The boat was anchored near the shore.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The workers secured the ladder to the wall.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory is based on earlier research.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: base 更宽泛；anchor 更形象，强调固定和稳定。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The claim is grounded in empirical data.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ground 和 anchor 都可指有依据；ground 更偏论证基础。

antonyms.example_sentence: The crisis destabilized the government.
antonyms.difference_note: destabilize 是破坏稳定，与 anchor 的稳固相反。

extension.root_affix: anchor 的具体物是“锚”，抽象迁移到“稳定基础”。

word_family.example_sentence: The belief is anchored in tradition.
The bay provided safe anchorage.

related_words.example_sentence: The theory is grounded in observation.

headline.core_en: an excessive supply of something, or to flood a market with too much of it
an excessive supply of something, or to flood a market with too much of it

familiar_bridge.summary: too much 是普通说法；glut 是市场或系统里东西太多，多到造成压力。

core.main: glut 常见于市场、商品、信息或劳动力供过于求。

usage_groups.group_title: 某物过量、供过于求
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 商品、信息或资源太多，超过需求或消化能力。

examples.sentence: A glut of cheap imports drove prices down.
examples.translation: 廉价进口商品过剩导致价格下跌。
examples.teaching_note: a glut of + 商品 表示供过于求。

examples.sentence: Streaming platforms have glutted the market with new shows.
examples.translation: 流媒体平台让市场充斥着大量新节目。
examples.teaching_note: 作动词时表示让市场过量充斥。

examples.sentence: A glut of cheap imports drove prices down.
examples.translation: 廉价进口商品过剩导致价格下跌。
examples.teaching_note: a glut of + 商品 表示供过于求。

examples.sentence: Streaming platforms have glutted the market with new shows.
examples.translation: 流媒体平台让市场充斥着大量新节目。
examples.teaching_note: 作动词时表示让市场过量充斥。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The farm produced a surplus of grain.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: surplus 中性，可指剩余；glut 常暗示过量造成问题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Excess sugar can harm health.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: excess 宽泛；glut 常用于市场或供应过剩。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Oversupply pushed prices lower.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: oversupply 更直接经济用语；glut 更生动，指大量过剩。

antonyms.example_sentence: A shortage of workers delayed the project.
antonyms.difference_note: shortage 是供应不足，与 glut 相反。

extension.root_affix: 重点记市场和信息语境中的“过量充斥”。

word_family.example_sentence: The market was glutted with unsold cars.
Cheap goods glutted the market.

related_words.example_sentence: The guests faced a surfeit of choices.

headline.core_cn: 面无表情的；一本正经的
headline.core_en: showing no emotion, especially while saying something funny

headline.core_cn: 面无表情的；一本正经的
headline.core_en: showing no emotion, especially while saying something funny

familiar_bridge.summary: dead + pan 可想成“脸像锅一样没表情”；deadpan 常用于冷面幽默。

core.main: deadpan 描写人说话或表演时表情平静、语气一本正经，常产生幽默效果。

usage_groups.group_title: 面无表情、一本正经地表达
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说话或表演时几乎不显露情绪，反而形成冷幽默效果。

examples.sentence: She told the absurd story in a deadpan voice.
examples.translation: 她用一本正经的语气讲了这个荒唐故事。
examples.teaching_note: deadpan voice/delivery 常指冷面幽默。

examples.sentence: The comedian’s deadpan delivery made the joke even funnier.
examples.translation: 这位喜剧演员面无表情的讲法让笑话更好笑。
examples.teaching_note: delivery 是喜剧表演中的“表达方式”。

examples.sentence: She told the absurd story in a deadpan voice.
examples.translation: 她用一本正经的语气讲了这个荒唐故事。
examples.teaching_note: deadpan voice/delivery 常指冷面幽默。

examples.sentence: The comedian’s deadpan delivery made the joke even funnier.
examples.translation: 这位喜剧演员面无表情的讲法让笑话更好笑。
examples.teaching_note: delivery 是喜剧表演中的“表达方式”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His face remained expressionless during the interview.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: expressionless 只是无表情；deadpan 常和幽默或讽刺有关。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She gave a straight-faced reply to the silly question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: straight-faced 和 deadpan 很接近，但 deadpan 更像表演风格。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The judge remained impassive during the testimony.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: impassive 强调不显露情绪；deadpan 常带幽默效果。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her expressive face made the story vivid.
antonyms.difference_note: expressive 与 deadpan 的面无表情相反。

extension.root_affix: deadpan 成词根，但 dead + pan 的画面可以帮助记：脸像平底锅一样没表情。

word_family.example_sentence: He answered deadpan, and everyone laughed.
She deadpanned the final line of the joke.

related_words.example_sentence: Her impassive reaction surprised the crowd.

headline.core_en: extremely delicate, light, or seeming not of this world

headline.core_cn: 轻盈飘逸的；超凡脱俗的
headline.core_en: extremely delicate, light, or seeming not of this world

familiar_bridge.summary: earthly 是尘世的；ethereal 像不太属于尘世，轻、淡、空灵。

core.main: ethereal 常形容声音、光线、气质或美感轻盈、空灵，仿佛不太真实。

usage_groups.group_title: 轻盈空灵、近乎超凡
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 声音、光线、人物气质或艺术效果显得轻、淡、飘忽。

examples.sentence: The singer’s ethereal voice seemed to float above the orchestra.

examples.sentence: The painting gives the landscape an ethereal quality.
examples.translation: 这幅画赋予风景一种超凡空灵的气质。
examples.teaching_note: art/description 中常说 ethereal quality。

examples.sentence: The singer’s ethereal voice seemed to float above the orchestra.

examples.sentence: The painting gives the landscape an ethereal quality.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The fabric was delicate and almost transparent.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The room felt airy and bright.

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The cave had an otherworldly glow.

headline.core_en: intense anger or violent force
intense anger or violent force

familiar_bridge.summary: anger 是普通生气；fury 是怒气或力量爆发到很强的程度。

core.main: fury 可以指人的狂怒，也可以指风暴、火焰等猛烈力量。

usage_groups.group_title: 强烈怒火或猛烈力量
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 情绪爆发，或自然力量猛烈到难以控制。

examples.sentence: She left the meeting in a fury after hearing the accusation.

examples.teaching_note: in a fury 表示处于盛怒中。

examples.sentence: The coastal town was unprepared for the fury of the storm.
examples.translation: 这个海滨小镇没有准备好应对风暴的猛烈袭击。

examples.sentence: She left the meeting in a fury after hearing the accusation.

examples.sentence: The coastal town was unprepared for the fury of the storm.

examples.teaching_note: fury 也可指自然力量的猛烈。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His anger was obvious, but he spoke calmly.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: anger 普通；fury 是更强烈、爆发性的怒气。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The crowd reacted with rage to the verdict.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: rage 和 fury 都强烈，rage 更偏情绪爆发。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy provoked the wrath of local residents.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: wrath 更书面、庄重，常带道德愤怒。

headline.core_en: a state of low energy, sluggishness, or lack of interest

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人、机构或社会显得迟缓、无力、提不起劲。

examples.sentence: After weeks of illness, he struggled with persistent lethargy.

headline.core_cn: 不可改变的；永恒不变的
headline.core_en: not able to be changed

headline.core_cn: 不可改变的；永恒不变的
headline.core_en: not able to be changed

familiar_bridge.summary: mutable 是可变的；immutable 加 im-，就是不可变。

core.main: immutable 强调规则、真理、性质或事实固定不变，不受时间或意愿影响。

usage_groups.group_title: 固定不变、无法改变
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 规则、原则、事实或性质被视为不可更改。

examples.sentence: The constitution was treated as an immutable law by its defenders.
examples.translation: 支持者把这部宪法视为不可更改的法律。
examples.teaching_note: immutable law 强调不能改动。

examples.sentence: The passage challenges the idea that social roles are immutable.
examples.translation: 文章质疑社会角色不可改变的观念。
examples.teaching_note: GRE 阅读中常用于批判“固定不变”的假设。

examples.sentence: The constitution was treated as an immutable law by its defenders.
examples.translation: 支持者把这部宪法视为不可更改的法律。

examples.sentence: The passage challenges the idea that social roles are immutable.
examples.translation: 文章质疑社会角色不可改变的观念。
examples.teaching_note: GRE 阅读中常用于批判“固定不变”的假设。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The village had an unchanging rhythm.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unchanging 普通；immutable 更强，几乎不能改变。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The schedule is fixed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fixed 可指安排确定；immutable 更抽象、更绝对。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The experiment assumes an invariable temperature.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: invariable 强调条件恒定；immutable 强调无法改变。

antonyms.example_sentence: Public opinion is mutable and often unpredictable.
antonyms.difference_note: mutable 是 immutable 的直接反义。

extension.root_affix: mut 表示变化，im- 表示否定；immutable 就是“不可变化”。

word_family.example_sentence: The rules are mutable rather than fixed.
The mutability of taste complicates prediction.
The mutation altered the organism.

related_words.example_sentence: Viruses can mutate rapidly.

headline.core_en: to persuade someone gently by flattery or coaxing
to persuade someone gently by flattery or coaxing

familiar_bridge.summary: persuade 是普通劝说；cajole 是用好话、讨好或软磨硬泡把人劝动。

core.main: cajole 强调不是强迫，而是用奉承、温和劝诱或软磨硬泡让人同意。

usage_groups.group_title: 用奉承或软磨硬泡劝动别人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说好话、讨好或耐心哄劝，让对方做某事。

examples.sentence: She cajoled her brother into helping with the project.
examples.translation: 她软磨硬泡地劝弟弟帮忙做项目。

examples.sentence: The lobbyist tried to cajole support from skeptical lawmakers.
examples.translation: 游说者试图从持怀疑态度的议员那里劝出支持。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于政治或谈判语境。

examples.sentence: She cajoled her brother into helping with the project.

examples.teaching_note: 可用于政治或谈判语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence persuaded many voters.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: persuade 中性；cajole 强调用好话或哄劝。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher coaxed the shy child to speak.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: coax 和 cajole 接近，coax 更温柔；cajole 可带操控感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He wheedled extra time from the supervisor.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: wheedle 比 cajole 更明显带狡猾讨好。

antonyms.example_sentence: The regime coerced citizens into silence.
antonyms.difference_note: coerce 是用压力强迫，和 cajole 的软性劝诱相反。

extension.root_affix: cajole ，重点记结构 cajole someone into doing something.

word_family.example_sentence: The sales pitch relied on cajolery.
His cajoling tone eventually softened her refusal.

related_words.example_sentence: She coaxed the cat out from under the bed.

headline.core_en: gloomy, sullen, and unwilling to be cheerful
gloomy, sullen, and unwilling to be cheerful

familiar_bridge.summary: sad 是普通难过；morose 更像长时间阴沉、不愿说话。

core.main: morose 形容人的情绪或气质阴沉、沉默、难以被安慰。

usage_groups.group_title: 阴沉闷闷不乐
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人的表情、态度或气氛低沉，不愿交流。

examples.sentence: He became morose after the failure and refused to discuss it.
examples.translation: 失败之后他变得闷闷不乐，拒绝谈这件事。
examples.teaching_note: morose 常带沉默、阴郁。

examples.sentence: The novel’s final chapters have a morose tone.
examples.translation: 小说最后几章带有阴郁的基调。
examples.teaching_note: 也可形容语气或氛围。

examples.sentence: He became morose after the failure and refused to discuss it.

examples.sentence: The novel’s final chapters have a morose tone.
examples.translation: 小说最后几章带有阴郁的基调。
examples.teaching_note: 也可形容语气或氛围。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She felt sad after hearing the news.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sad 普通；morose 更阴沉、持续，也更不愿交流。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The room felt gloomy in the rain.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: gloomy 可形容天气/房间/情绪；morose 更常形容人。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The child gave a sullen reply.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sullen 强调不高兴且不愿说话；morose 更沉郁。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her cheerful mood lifted the room.
antonyms.difference_note: cheerful 与 morose 的阴沉相反。

extension.root_affix: 可和 gloomy, sullen 一起记语气。

word_family.example_sentence: He stared morosely out the window.
His moroseness worried his friends.

related_words.example_sentence: A melancholy mood pervades the poem.

headline.core_en: to object politely or hesitate to agree
to object politely or hesitate to agree

familiar_bridge.summary: object 是反对；demur 更像礼貌地提出异议或迟疑不肯同意。

core.main: demur 常用于正式语境，表示对建议、要求或判断提出保留意见。

usage_groups.group_title: 礼貌地表示异议或迟疑
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不是激烈反对，而是提出保留、不同意或不愿马上接受。

examples.sentence: Several members demurred at the cost of the plan.
examples.translation: 几位成员对该计划的成本表示异议。
examples.teaching_note: demur at 常接让人犹豫或反对的对象。

examples.sentence: She accepted the difficult assignment without demur.
examples.translation: 她毫无异议地接受了这项困难任务。
examples.teaching_note: without demur 表示没有反对。

examples.sentence: Several members demurred at the cost of the plan.
examples.translation: 几位成员对该计划的成本表示异议。
examples.teaching_note: demur at 常接让人犹豫或反对的对象。

examples.sentence: She accepted the difficult assignment without demur.
examples.translation: 她毫无异议地接受了这项困难任务。
examples.teaching_note: without demur 表示没有反对。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The residents objected to the new road.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: object 普通；demur 更委婉、正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He hesitated before signing the contract.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: hesitate 只是犹豫；demur 通常包含异议。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The advisor remonstrated with the king.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: remonstrate 更强烈，常带劝诫；demur 更克制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The board assented to the proposal.
antonyms.difference_note: assent 是正式同意，与 demur 相反。

extension.root_affix: demur ，重点记正式搭配 without demur / demur at.

word_family.example_sentence: His demurral delayed the vote.
Her demurring tone suggested doubt.

related_words.example_sentence: The objection was noted in the record.

headline.core_en: annoying, irritating, or troublesome
annoying, irritating, or troublesome

familiar_bridge.summary: nettle 是会刺人的荨麻；nettlesome 就像小刺一样让人烦、难处理。

core.main: nettlesome 常形容问题、任务、争议或细节让人烦恼且难处理。

usage_groups.group_title: 烦人又棘手
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 问题不一定巨大，但持续刺人、干扰解决。

examples.sentence: The committee faced a nettlesome problem about funding.
examples.translation: 委员会遇到了一个棘手的资金问题。

examples.sentence: The essay avoids the nettlesome question of who should pay for reform.
examples.translation: 这篇文章回避了谁该为改革买单这个棘手问题。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于复杂争议或难题。

examples.sentence: The committee faced a nettlesome problem about funding.

examples.sentence: The essay avoids the nettlesome question of who should pay for reform.
examples.translation: 这篇文章回避了谁该为改革买单这个棘手问题。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于复杂争议或难题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The noise was annoying during the lecture.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: annoying 普通；nettlesome 更像问题带刺、难处理。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The rule created troublesome delays.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: troublesome 强调造成麻烦；nettlesome 更强调烦人且棘手。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The inconsistency remained a vexing puzzle.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vexing 更正式，和 nettlesome 接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The repair was straightforward and quick.
antonyms.difference_note: straightforward 与 nettlesome 的棘手相反。

extension.root_affix: nettle 是荨麻，也可作动词“激怒”。nettlesome 保留“被刺到、烦人”的感觉。

word_family.example_sentence: Her dismissive tone nettled him.
He looked nettled by the criticism.

related_words.example_sentence: The delay vexed the travelers.

headline.core_en: something that is unimportant, or the state of being trivial
something that is unimportant, or the state of being trivial

familiar_bridge.summary: trivial 是形容词；triviality 是“琐碎之事”或“不重要性”。

core.main: triviality 常指不值得耗费注意力的小事，也可指某事缺乏重要性。

usage_groups.group_title: 琐碎小事或不重要性
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 讨论被小事占据，或强调某事并非小事。

examples.sentence: The meeting wasted an hour on trivialities.
examples.translation: 会议在琐事上浪费了一个小时。
examples.teaching_note: trivialities 是复数，指一堆小事。

examples.sentence: The author insists that the issue is not a triviality but a moral problem.
examples.translation: 作者坚持认为这个问题不是小事，而是道德问题。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于反驳“这不重要”的看法。

examples.sentence: The meeting wasted an hour on trivialities.

examples.teaching_note: trivialities 是复数，指一堆小事。

examples.sentence: The author insists that the issue is not a triviality but a moral problem.
examples.translation: 作者坚持认为这个问题不是小事，而是道德问题。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于反驳“这不重要”的看法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The delay was a small matter compared with the larger crisis.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: small matter 更口语；triviality 更正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The argument turns on a small detail.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: detail 可重要可不重要；triviality 通常不重要。

antonyms.example_sentence: The court treated the case as a matter of consequence.
antonyms.difference_note: matter of consequence 与 triviality 相反。

extension.root_affix: triviality 是 trivial 的名词形式，和 trivia/trivialize 同族。

familiar_bridge.summary: trivia 是有趣的小知识，但通常不是关键知识。

core.main: trivia 多指零散、好玩但不重要的信息，常见于游戏、娱乐或批评文章太琐碎。

usage_groups.group_title: 有趣但不重要的零散事实

headline.core_en: to make something seem less important or serious than it really is
to make something seem less important or serious than it really is

familiar_bridge.summary: trivial 是微不足道；trivialize 就是把本来严肃的事说得像小事。

core.main: trivialize 常批评某种表达淡化痛苦、风险、历史或严肃问题。

usage_groups.group_title: 把严肃问题说成小事
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 降低某事的重要性或严重性，常带批评。

examples.sentence: The advertisement trivialized the illness by treating it as a joke.
examples.translation: 这则广告把疾病当笑话处理，从而淡化了其严重性。
examples.teaching_note: trivialize an illness/problem 带批评。

examples.sentence: The author warns that simple comparisons can trivialize historical trauma.
examples.translation: 作者警告说，简单类比可能会淡化历史创伤。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于伦理、历史和社会议题。

examples.sentence: The advertisement trivialized the illness by treating it as a joke.
examples.translation: 这则广告把疾病当笑话处理，从而淡化了其严重性。
examples.teaching_note: trivialize an illness/problem 带批评。

examples.sentence: The author warns that simple comparisons can trivialize historical trauma.
examples.translation: 作者警告说，简单类比可能会淡化历史创伤。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于伦理、历史和社会议题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The official downplayed the risks of the plan.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: downplay 可策略性降低重要性；trivialize 更强调把严肃事物说成小事。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report minimized the scale of the damage.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: minimize 强调缩小程度；trivialize 强调降低严肃性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He belittled her contribution to the project.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: belittle 常针对人或贡献；trivialize 常针对问题或痛苦。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speaker emphasized the seriousness of the crisis.
antonyms.difference_note: emphasize 与 trivialize 的淡化相反。

extension.root_affix: trivialize = trivial + -ize，使某事变得像 trivial 一样不重要。

The argument is not a triviality.

headline.core_en: excessively proud of oneself and eager for admiration
excessively proud of oneself and eager for admiration

familiar_bridge.summary: vain 是虚荣；glory 是荣耀。vainglorious 就是沉迷于炫耀自己的荣耀。

core.main: vainglorious 形容人过度自夸、追求赞美，语气强烈负面。

examples.sentence: The general’s vainglorious speech exaggerated his role in the victory.

examples.teaching_note: vainglorious speech/boasting 带强烈贬义。

examples.sentence: The memoir is less a confession than a vainglorious display of achievement.
examples.translation: 这本回忆录与其说是忏悔，不如说是对成就的虚荣展示。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于批评自我美化。

examples.sentence: The general’s vainglorious speech exaggerated his role in the victory.
examples.translation: 将军自负夸耀的演说夸大了自己在胜利中的作用。
examples.teaching_note: vainglorious speech/boasting 带强烈贬义。

examples.sentence: The memoir is less a confession than a vainglorious display of achievement.
examples.translation: 这本回忆录与其说是忏悔，不如说是对成就的虚荣展示。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于批评自我美化。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His boastful tone annoyed the audience.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: boastful 普通；vainglorious 更书面、更强烈。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She was too vain to admit the mistake.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: vain 可指爱美或自负；vainglorious 更强调追求荣耀和赞美。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay criticizes the author’s egotistical style.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: egotistical 强调自我中心；vainglorious 强调炫耀荣耀。

antonyms.example_sentence: The scientist remained humble despite her fame.
antonyms.difference_note: humble 与 vainglorious 的自夸相反。

extension.root_affix: vain 表示虚荣、徒劳；glory 是荣耀。vainglorious 可以理解成“虚荣地追求荣耀”。

word_family.example_sentence: The poem mocks the king’s vainglory.
His vain attempt failed quickly.
They remembered a glorious victory.

related_words.example_sentence: He boasted about his success.

headline.core_en: to cut away unnecessary parts, especially branches or extra material
to cut away unnecessary parts, especially branches or extra material

familiar_bridge.summary: prune 可以是修剪树枝，也可以是删掉文章、预算或名单中的多余部分。

core.main: prune 的核心是去掉多余部分，让整体更健康、更清楚或更可控。

usage_groups.group_title: 剪掉多余部分
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 从植物、文本、预算或计划中去除不需要的部分。

examples.sentence: The gardener pruned the branches to help the tree grow stronger.
examples.translation: 园丁修剪枝条，让树长得更健壮。
examples.teaching_note: 具体义是修剪植物。

examples.sentence: The editor pruned unnecessary details from the manuscript.
examples.translation: 编辑删去了手稿中不必要的细节。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象义是删减文字或内容。

examples.sentence: The gardener pruned the branches to help the tree grow stronger.

examples.sentence: The editor pruned unnecessary details from the manuscript.
examples.translation: 编辑删去了手稿中不必要的细节。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象义是删减文字或内容。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She trimmed the hedge along the path.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trim 更普通；prune 常强调去掉多余部分以改善整体。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The director cut several scenes.

headline.core_en: a place or supply where something is stored for future use
a place or supply where something is stored for future use

familiar_bridge.summary: reserve 是保留；reservoir 是储存起来的一大池东西，可以是水，也可以是知识、情感或资源。

core.main: reservoir 具体是水库，抽象上是某种东西的储备来源。

usage_groups.group_title: 储存某物的地方或来源
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 水、资源、知识、情感或病毒等被积聚、保存。

examples.sentence: The city built a reservoir to store water for the dry season.
examples.translation: 这座城市修建水库，为旱季储水。
examples.teaching_note: 具体义是水库。

examples.sentence: The library became a reservoir of local knowledge.
examples.translation: 这座图书馆成了地方知识的储备库。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象义表示知识或资源的积累处。

examples.sentence: The city built a reservoir to store water for the dry season.

examples.sentence: The library became a reservoir of local knowledge.

examples.teaching_note: 抽象义表示知识或资源的积累处。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The attic became a store of old furniture.

antonyms.example_sentence: The drought created a shortage of clean water.

headline.core_en: related to beauty, art, or the appreciation of appearance
related to beauty, art, or the appreciation of appearance

familiar_bridge.summary: beauty 是美；aesthetic 是关于美感、艺术感或审美判断的。

core.main: aesthetic 可作形容词，也可作名词，指某种审美风格或原则。

usage_groups.group_title: 关于美感、艺术和外观判断
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 评价作品、设计、风格或体验的美感。

examples.sentence: The building has aesthetic appeal as well as practical value.
examples.translation: 这座建筑既有实用价值，也有审美吸引力。
examples.teaching_note: aesthetic appeal 是常见表达。

examples.sentence: The artist rejected the dominant aesthetic of her time.
examples.translation: 这位艺术家拒绝了她所处时代的主流审美。
examples.teaching_note: 作名词时可指审美风格。

examples.sentence: The building has aesthetic appeal as well as practical value.
examples.translation: 这座建筑既有实用价值，也有审美吸引力。

examples.sentence: The artist rejected the dominant aesthetic of her time.

examples.teaching_note: 作名词时可指审美风格。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The film has strong artistic ambition.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: artistic 偏艺术创作；aesthetic 偏美感和审美评价。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The garden is beautiful in spring.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: beautiful 是直接评价；aesthetic 更抽象、更学术。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic focused on stylistic differences between the poems.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: stylistic 强调风格；aesthetic 更宽，含美感原则。

antonyms.example_sentence: The design is utilitarian rather than decorative.
antonyms.difference_note: utilitarian 强调用途，常和 aesthetic 的美感维度对照。

extension.root_affix: aesthetic 和 aesthetics 同族；都围绕 beauty/art/perception。

source.source_meaning_cn: 提示， 提 词； 提醒

headline.core_en: to cause someone to act or to give a cue; quick and immediate
to cause someone to act or to give a cue; quick and immediate

familiar_bridge.summary: prompt 可以是“提示”，也可以是“促使”。作为形容词则是“迅速的”。

core.main: prompt 的核心是让某事马上发生：提示某人、引发反应，或行动迅速。

usage_groups.group_title: 提示或促使某事发生
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 问题、事件、证据或提示让人回应或行动。

examples.sentence: The teacher’s question prompted a lively discussion.
examples.translation: 老师的问题引发了热烈讨论。
examples.teaching_note: prompt 作动词表示促使反应出现。

examples.sentence: The crisis required prompt action from local officials.
examples.translation: 这场危机需要地方官员迅速行动。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词 prompt 表示迅速及时。

examples.sentence: The teacher’s question prompted a lively discussion.
examples.translation: 老师的问题引发了热烈讨论。
examples.teaching_note: prompt 作动词表示促使反应出现。

examples.sentence: The crisis required prompt action from local officials.
examples.translation: 这场危机需要地方官员迅速行动。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词 prompt 表示迅速及时。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm caused widespread delays.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: cause 宽泛；prompt 更强调触发反应或行动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The note reminded her to call the doctor.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: remind 是提醒记起；prompt 可提醒，也可引发行动。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The question elicited a thoughtful answer.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: elicit 强调把回答或反应引出来；prompt 强调促发行动或回应。

antonyms.example_sentence: The dispute delayed the decision.
antonyms.difference_note: delay 是使变慢或推迟，与 prompt 的迅速促发相反。

extension.root_affix: prompt 不是高价值硬拆词，重点按词性记：prompt a response / prompt action / writing prompt。

The promptness of the reply surprised us.

headline.core_en: to guess something based on limited evidence
to guess something based on limited evidence

familiar_bridge.summary: guess 是猜；surmise 是根据有限线索作出较理性的推测。

core.main: surmise 常在证据不足但有线索时使用，语气比 guess 更正式。

usage_groups.group_title: 根据有限线索推测
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 没有完整证据，但从迹象中作出判断。

examples.sentence: From the empty shelves, she surmised that the store was closing.
examples.translation: 看到空货架，她推测这家店要关门了。
examples.teaching_note: surmise from evidence 表示根据线索判断。

examples.sentence: The historian can only surmise what motivated the decision.
examples.translation: 历史学家只能推测这一决定的动机。
examples.teaching_note: can only surmise 强调证据有限。

examples.sentence: From the empty shelves, she surmised that the store was closing.
examples.translation: 看到空货架，她推测这家店要关门了。
examples.teaching_note: surmise from evidence 表示根据线索判断。

examples.sentence: The historian can only surmise what motivated the decision.
examples.translation: 历史学家只能推测这一决定的动机。
examples.teaching_note: can only surmise 强调证据有限。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: I guessed that he was tired.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: guess 可随意；surmise 更基于线索。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: We can infer the author’s attitude from the final paragraph.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: infer 更强调逻辑推断；surmise 证据更有限。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Scholars conjecture that the text is incomplete.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conjecture 更学术，常指理论性推测。

antonyms.example_sentence: We know the date from official records.

extension.root_affix: surmise ，重点和 infer / conjecture 做层次比较。

word_family.example_sentence: His conclusion was only a surmise.

headline.core_en: trust, praise, or recognition given to someone or something
trust, praise, or recognition given to someone or something

familiar_bridge.summary: cred 这组词都和 believe/trust 有关；credit 可以是信用，也可以是把功劳归给某人。

core.main: credit 的几个义项都围绕“相信、认可、归功”。

usage_groups.group_title: 相信、认可或归功
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 可指金融信用、名誉认可，也可指把成就归给某人。

examples.sentence: The scientist was given credit for the discovery.
examples.translation: 这位科学家因这项发现获得了认可。
examples.teaching_note: give credit for 表示归功或认可。

examples.sentence: Banks use credit scores to judge whether borrowers are reliable.
examples.translation: 银行用信用评分判断借款人是否可靠。
examples.teaching_note: 金融义仍然和 trust 有关。

examples.sentence: The scientist was given credit for the discovery.
examples.translation: 这位科学家因这项发现获得了认可。
examples.teaching_note: give credit for 表示归功或认可。

examples.sentence: Banks use credit scores to judge whether borrowers are reliable.
examples.translation: 银行用信用评分判断借款人是否可靠。
examples.teaching_note: 金融义仍然和 trust 有关。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher praised the student’s careful work.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: praise 是称赞；credit 更强调认可贡献或归功。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The company lost the public’s trust.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trust 是相信；credit 在金融或名誉语境中包含信任。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The success was attributed to careful planning.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: attribute 更正式，强调把原因或功劳归给某人/某物。

antonyms.example_sentence: The mayor blamed the failure on poor planning.
antonyms.difference_note: blame 是归咎；credit 是归功。

extension.root_affix: cred 表示 believe/trust。credit, credible, credulous, credence, credential, discredit 都可成组记。

word_family.example_sentence: The witness gave a credible account.
The rumor gained credence.
Her credentials impressed the committee.
The scandal discredited the official.

related_words.example_sentence: The movement was guided by a simple creed.

source.source_meaning_cn: 可 信 的； 令 人 信服 的

headline.core_en: able to be believed or trusted
able to be believed or trusted

familiar_bridge.summary: cred 是 believe/trust；credible 就是“值得相信”。

core.main: credible 常形容证据、解释、证人、威胁或说法可信。

usage_groups.group_title: 值得相信或信任
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 证据、解释、证人或说法足够可靠。

examples.sentence: The journalist found a credible source for the story.
examples.translation: 记者为这篇报道找到了可信来源。
examples.teaching_note: credible source 指信息来源值得相信。

examples.sentence: The theory became more credible after new evidence appeared.
examples.translation: 新证据出现后，这一理论变得更可信。
examples.teaching_note: 学术语境中常评价 theory/evidence。

examples.sentence: The journalist found a credible source for the story.
examples.translation: 记者为这篇报道找到了可信来源。
examples.teaching_note: credible source 指信息来源值得相信。

examples.sentence: The theory became more credible after new evidence appeared.
examples.translation: 新证据出现后，这一理论变得更可信。
examples.teaching_note: 学术语境中常评价 theory/evidence。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her explanation sounded believable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: believable 更日常；credible 更正式，可用于证据和来源。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The data came from a reliable source.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reliable 强调稳定可靠；credible 强调能否被相信。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The hypothesis is plausible but unproven.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: plausible 是看起来合理；credible 更强调可信度。

antonyms.example_sentence: The claim sounded incredible without evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: incredible 可表示惊人或不可信，和 credible 方向相反。

extension.root_affix: cred = believe/trust；credible 是“可相信的”。

word_family.example_sentence: The scandal damaged the report’s credibility.
The evidence lent credence to the claim.
The error discredited the study.

headline.core_en: too ready to believe things without enough evidence
too ready to believe things without enough evidence

familiar_bridge.summary: credible 是“可信”；credulous 是“太容易相信”。

core.main: credulous 描写人缺少怀疑精神，容易相信不可靠的说法。

usage_groups.group_title: 太容易相信别人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人对传言、骗局或荒唐说法缺少判断。

examples.sentence: The scam targeted credulous investors who wanted quick profits.
examples.translation: 这个骗局针对的是想快速获利、容易轻信的投资者。
examples.teaching_note: credulous investors 是常见语境。

examples.sentence: A credulous reader might accept the story without asking for evidence.
examples.translation: 轻信的读者可能不要求证据就接受这个故事。
examples.teaching_note: 强调缺少批判判断。

examples.sentence: The scam targeted credulous investors who wanted quick profits.
examples.translation: 这个骗局针对的是想快速获利、容易轻信的投资者。
examples.teaching_note: credulous investors 是常见语境。

examples.sentence: A credulous reader might accept the story without asking for evidence.
examples.translation: 轻信的读者可能不要求证据就接受这个故事。
examples.teaching_note: 强调缺少批判判断。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Gullible tourists paid for fake tickets.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: gullible 更口语，常指被骗；credulous 更书面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The child was trusting of strangers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trusting 可中性或正面；credulous 通常负面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The naive buyer believed every promise.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: naive 强调经验不足；credulous 强调过度相信。

antonyms.example_sentence: A skeptical audience demanded evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: skeptical 是持怀疑态度，与 credulous 相反。

extension.root_affix: cred = believe/trust；credulous 是“太愿意相信”。不要和 credible（可信的）混。

word_family.example_sentence: The fraud depended on public credulity.
The claim is credible only with evidence.
She gave him an incredulous look.

related_words.example_sentence: Gullible buyers believed the advertisement.

headline.core_en: belief or acceptance that something is true
belief or acceptance that something is true

familiar_bridge.summary: credence 不是 credit，而是“给予某说法相信”。

core.main: credence 常见结构是 give/lend credence to，表示让某观点更可信。

usage_groups.group_title: 使某说法获得相信
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 证据、事实或权威让某个说法更有可信度。

examples.sentence: The new documents lend credence to her account of the event.
examples.translation: 新文件使她对事件的叙述更可信。

examples.sentence: Few historians give credence to the legend.

examples.sentence: The new documents lend credence to her account of the event.
examples.translation: 新文件使她对事件的叙述更可信。

examples.sentence: Few historians give credence to the legend.

examples.teaching_note: give credence to 表示相信或接受。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His belief in the story faded over time.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Public trust in the agency declined.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trust 更偏对人或机构；credence 更偏对说法、理论。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The idea gained acceptance among scientists.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: acceptance 强调被接受；credence 强调被相信为真。

antonyms.example_sentence: The contradiction cast doubt on the testimony.

The forgery discredited the theory.

headline.core_en: a qualification or document proving someone’s identity, authority, or ability
a qualification or document proving someone’s identity, authority, or ability

familiar_bridge.summary: cred 仍是 trust：credential 是让别人相信你有资格的证明。

core.main: credential 常指学历、证书、资历或身份凭据，用来证明某人可靠或有资格。

usage_groups.group_title: 证明资格或身份的凭据
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 证书、学历、资历或身份文件让别人相信你有能力或权限。

examples.sentence: The hospital checks every doctor’s credentials before hiring.
examples.translation: 医院在聘用前会核查每位医生的资质。
examples.teaching_note: professional credentials 指专业资历。

examples.sentence: Users must enter their login credentials to access the database.
examples.translation: 用户必须输入登录凭据才能访问数据库。
examples.teaching_note: 现代语境中也指账号凭据。

examples.sentence: The hospital checks every doctor’s credentials before hiring.
examples.translation: 医院在聘用前会核查每位医生的资质。
examples.teaching_note: professional credentials 指专业资历。

examples.sentence: Users must enter their login credentials to access the database.
examples.translation: 用户必须输入登录凭据才能访问数据库。
examples.teaching_note: 现代语境中也指账号凭据。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her qualifications matched the job requirements.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: qualification 指资格本身；credential 常是证明资格的文件或资历。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The course provides a certificate of completion.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: certificate 是具体证书；credential 范围更广。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The program received official accreditation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: accreditation 强调机构正式认可；credential 可指个人资历。

headline.core_en: simple and natural, without skillful deception or artificiality

headline.core_cn: 朴拙的；不做作的；天真的
headline.core_en: simple and natural, without skillful deception or artificiality

familiar_bridge.summary: art 可以是技巧；artless 不是“没有艺术”，而是没有做作或心机。

core.main: artless 可褒义指自然真诚，也可指缺乏技巧、显得笨拙。

usage_groups.group_title: 自然不做作，或缺少技巧
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人、表达或风格没有精心伪装，有时真诚，有时笨拙。

examples.sentence: Her artless honesty made the apology convincing.
examples.translation: 她不做作的坦诚让道歉显得可信。
examples.teaching_note: artless 可正面：自然真诚。

examples.sentence: The early poems are artless but emotionally direct.
examples.translation: 这些早期诗作技巧朴拙，但情感直接。
examples.teaching_note: 也可表示技巧不成熟。

examples.sentence: Her artless honesty made the apology convincing.

examples.teaching_note: artless 可正面：自然真诚。

examples.sentence: The early poems are artless but emotionally direct.
examples.translation: 这些早期诗作技巧朴拙，但情感直接。
examples.teaching_note: 也可表示技巧不成熟。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His natural manner put people at ease.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: natural 普通；artless 强调没有做作或心机。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The design is simple and clear.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: simple 中性；artless 可带真诚或笨拙。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her ingenuous question revealed real curiosity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ingenuous 更强调天真坦率；artless 还可指缺少技巧。

antonyms.example_sentence: His affected accent sounded unnatural.
antonyms.difference_note: affected 与 artless 的自然不做作相反。

extension.root_affix: artless = art + -less。这里 art 更接近“技巧/心机”，不是简单的艺术。

word_family.example_sentence: The artlessness of the letter made it moving.
The negotiator made an artful concession.
The performance avoided artifice.

related_words.example_sentence: His ingenuous reply disarmed the critics.

headline.core_en: to represent or give concrete form to an idea, quality, or principle
to represent or give concrete form to an idea, quality, or principle

familiar_bridge.summary: body 是身体；embody 像给抽象思想一个“身体”，让它具体呈现出来。

core.main: embody 常用于人、制度、作品或行动体现某种价值、原则或特征。

usage_groups.group_title: 把抽象品质具体表现出来
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人、作品、制度或行为成为某种思想或价值的具体呈现。

examples.sentence: The leader’s actions embodied the movement’s ideals.
examples.translation: 这位领导人的行动体现了运动的理想。
examples.teaching_note: embody ideals/values 是核心用法。

examples.sentence: The building embodies the tension between tradition and modernity.
examples.translation: 这座建筑体现了传统与现代之间的张力。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于艺术、建筑、理论分析。

examples.sentence: The leader’s actions embodied the movement’s ideals.
examples.translation: 这位领导人的行动体现了运动的理想。
examples.teaching_note: embody ideals/values 是核心用法。

examples.sentence: The building embodies the tension between tradition and modernity.
examples.translation: 这座建筑体现了传统与现代之间的张力。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于艺术、建筑、理论分析。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The flag represents national unity.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: represent 更宽；embody 强调把抽象品质具体化。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The results show a clear pattern.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: show 普通；embody 更强调成为某种品质的载体。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The case epitomizes the problem of inequality.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: epitomize 更强调典型代表；embody 强调具体承载。

antonyms.example_sentence: His behavior contradicted his stated values.
antonyms.difference_note: contradict 表示背离价值，与 embody 形成对照。

extension.root_affix: em-/en- 可表示“使成为”；body 是身体。embody 就是让抽象东西有具体形体。

word_family.example_sentence: She is the embodiment of patience.
The values are embodied in the law.

related_words.example_sentence: The hero seems to incarnate courage.

headline.core_en: causing worry or concern
causing worry or concern

familiar_bridge.summary: worry 是担心；worrisome 是让人担心的情况或迹象。

core.main: worrisome 常形容趋势、症状、迹象或发展让人产生担忧。

usage_groups.group_title: 让人担心的迹象或趋势
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某种变化、症状或消息暗示可能有问题。

examples.sentence: The sudden drop in attendance is a worrisome sign.
examples.translation: 出席人数突然下降是一个令人担忧的信号。
examples.teaching_note: worrisome sign 指让人开始担心的迹象。

examples.sentence: Doctors described the new symptoms as worrisome but not conclusive.
examples.translation: 医生认为这些新症状令人担忧，但还不能下结论。
examples.teaching_note: 医学或风险评估中常用。

examples.sentence: The sudden drop in attendance is a worrisome sign.
examples.translation: 出席人数突然下降是一个令人担忧的信号。
examples.teaching_note: worrisome sign 指让人开始担心的迹象。

examples.sentence: Doctors described the new symptoms as worrisome but not conclusive.
examples.translation: 医生认为这些新症状令人担忧，但还不能下结论。
examples.teaching_note: 医学或风险评估中常用。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The delay is concerning for investors.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: concerning 和 worrisome 接近，concerning 稍正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report revealed troubling patterns.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: troubling 更偏令人不安；worrisome 更直接让人担心。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The alarming increase in cases prompted action.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: alarming 比 worrisome 更强，表示需要警觉。

antonyms.example_sentence: The test results were reassuring.
antonyms.difference_note: reassuring 与 worrisome 相反。

extension.root_affix: worrisome = worry + -some，表示“会引起担忧的”。

word_family.example_sentence: Parents worry about safety.
She looked worried after the call.
The trend is worrying.

related_words.example_sentence: The data raised concern.

headline.core_en: a statement or assumption on which an argument or story is based
a statement or assumption on which an argument or story is based

familiar_bridge.summary: pre- 是在前；premise 是论证或故事开始时先放下的基础。

core.main: premise 可以是推理前提，也可以是故事、理论或计划的基本设定。

usage_groups.group_title: 论证或故事赖以成立的基础设定
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 结论、理论或情节建立在这个假设之上。

examples.sentence: The argument rests on the premise that people act rationally.
examples.translation: 这个论证建立在人会理性行动这一前提上。
examples.teaching_note: premise of an argument 是核心用法。

examples.sentence: The film’s premise is simple: one decision changes an entire life.
examples.translation: 这部电影的设定很简单：一个决定改变整个人生。
examples.teaching_note: 故事设定也可叫 premise。

examples.sentence: The argument rests on the premise that people act rationally.
examples.translation: 这个论证建立在人会理性行动这一前提上。
examples.teaching_note: premise of an argument 是核心用法。

examples.sentence: The film’s premise is simple: one decision changes an entire life.
examples.translation: 这部电影的设定很简单：一个决定改变整个人生。
examples.teaching_note: 故事设定也可叫 premise。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The calculation depends on several assumptions.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: assumption 是假设；premise 更常是论证基础。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision has a legal basis.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: basis 更宽泛；premise 是论证或故事的基础命题。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The theory begins with a simple postulate.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: postulate 更理论化，常指先行假定。

antonyms.example_sentence: The conclusion follows from the premise.
antonyms.difference_note: conclusion 是由 premise 推出的结果。

extension.root_affix: pre- 表示在前；premise 是推理前面先放下的东西。

word_family.example_sentence: The policy is premised on continued growth.
The premise of the novel is unusual.

related_words.example_sentence: The argument contains a hidden presupposition.

headline.core_cn: 进展；逐步推进；连续发展
headline.core_en: a gradual movement or development from one stage to another

headline.core_cn: 进展；逐步推进；连续发展
headline.core_en: a gradual movement or development from one stage to another

familiar_bridge.summary: progress 是进步；progression 更强调一步一步的顺序推进。

core.main: progression 常指事件、阶段、疾病、音乐和思想按顺序发展。

usage_groups.group_title: 按阶段逐步发展
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 从一个阶段推进到下一个阶段，有顺序和过程感。

examples.sentence: The course shows a clear progression from basic concepts to complex debates.
examples.translation: 这门课程清楚地从基本概念推进到复杂争论。

examples.sentence: Doctors monitored the progression of the disease over several months.
examples.translation: 医生连续几个月监测疾病的发展。
examples.teaching_note: 医学语境中指病情进展。

examples.sentence: The course shows a clear progression from basic concepts to complex debates.
examples.translation: 这门课程清楚地从基本概念推进到复杂争论。

examples.sentence: Doctors monitored the progression of the disease over several months.
examples.translation: 医生连续几个月监测疾病的发展。
examples.teaching_note: 医学语境中指病情进展。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The development of the theory took decades.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: development 宽泛；progression 强调阶段顺序。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The advance of technology changed daily life.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: advance 强调向前；progression 更强调过程链条。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The sequence of events is hard to reconstruct.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sequence 强调顺序；progression 强调发展推进。

antonyms.example_sentence: The data show regression rather than improvement.
antonyms.difference_note: regression 与 progression 的向前发展相反。

extension.root_affix: pro- 有向前的方向；progression 和 progress/progressive 成组记。

word_family.example_sentence: The project made steady progress.
The policy introduced progressive reforms.
The model detected regression in performance.

related_words.example_sentence: The sequence matters in the experiment.

source.source_meaning_cn: 唤 起；使 想到；使 想起

headline.core_en: to bring a feeling, memory, or image into someone’s mind
to bring a feeling, memory, or image into someone’s mind

familiar_bridge.summary: voice/voke 有“叫出”的感觉；evoke 就是把记忆、情绪或画面“唤出来”。

core.main: evoke 常用于艺术、气味、文字、场景引发记忆、情绪或联想。

usage_groups.group_title: 唤起记忆、情绪或画面
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某种声音、气味、文字或景象让内心反应出现。

examples.sentence: The smell of rain evoked memories of her childhood village.
examples.translation: 雨的气味唤起了她对童年村庄的记忆。

examples.sentence: The poem evokes a sense of loss without naming it directly.
examples.translation: 这首诗没有直接说出失落，却唤起了一种失落感。
examples.teaching_note: 文学语境中常表示引发感受。

examples.sentence: The smell of rain evoked memories of her childhood village.
examples.translation: 雨的气味唤起了她对童年村庄的记忆。

examples.sentence: The poem evokes a sense of loss without naming it directly.
examples.translation: 这首诗没有直接说出失落，却唤起了一种失落感。
examples.teaching_note: 文学语境中常表示引发感受。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The song brought back memories of school.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: bring back 更口语；evoke 更正式、更文学。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The photograph reminded him of home.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: remind 常接人想起某事；evoke 更强调情感或意象被唤起。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The description conjured an image of a deserted city.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conjure 更有画面突然浮现的感觉。

antonyms.example_sentence: He tried to suppress the painful memory.
antonyms.difference_note: suppress 是压下去，和 evoke 的唤起相反。

extension.root_affix: voc/voke 和“叫、声音”有关。evoke 是把情绪或记忆叫出来。

source.source_meaning_cn: 唤起 感情 的； 产生 共鸣 的； 引起 回忆 的

headline.core_en: bringing strong images, memories, or feelings to mind
bringing strong images, memories, or feelings to mind

familiar_bridge.summary: evoke 是动词；evocative 是能唤起记忆、画面或情绪的。

core.main: evocative 常形容音乐、文字、气味、图像或场景让人产生强烈联想。

usage_groups.group_title: 能唤起记忆、画面或情绪
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 艺术、语言或感官细节让人想到某种经历或氛围。

examples.sentence: The novel opens with an evocative description of the sea.
examples.translation: 小说开头对海的描写很有画面感，能唤起强烈感受。
examples.teaching_note: evocative description 强调引发画面和情绪。

examples.sentence: For many readers, the smell of smoke is evocative of winter evenings.
examples.translation: 对许多读者来说，烟味会唤起冬夜的感觉。
examples.teaching_note: evocative of 表示让人想到某种经历。

examples.sentence: The novel opens with an evocative description of the sea.
examples.translation: 小说开头对海的描写很有画面感，能唤起强烈感受。
examples.teaching_note: evocative description 强调引发画面和情绪。

examples.sentence: For many readers, the smell of smoke is evocative of winter evenings.
examples.translation: 对许多读者来说，烟味会唤起冬夜的感觉。
examples.teaching_note: evocative of 表示让人想到某种经历。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The title is suggestive of a hidden conflict.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: suggestive 强调暗示；evocative 强调唤起感受或画面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her expressive voice carried the scene.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: expressive 强调表达情感；evocative 强调引发读者/听者感受。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 引起共鸣的；意味深长的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The phrase became resonant after the crisis.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: resonant 更强调共鸣和深层意义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The flat description failed to move readers.
antonyms.difference_note: flat 缺乏感染力，与 evocative 相反。

extension.root_affix: evocative 来自 evoke；-ative 表示具有某种作用。

word_family.example_sentence: The song evoked memories of home.
The passage is an evocation of childhood fear.
The scene is evocatively written.

related_words.example_sentence: The image remained resonant for years.

source.source_note: From archived Unit 2 OCR extraction; OCR text remains internal evidence only.
source.source_meaning_cn: 停止； 停顿； 停滞

headline.core_en: a state in which movement or progress has completely stopped
a state in which movement or progress has completely stopped

familiar_bridge.summary: stand still 字面是站着不动；standstill 是进程、交通、谈判完全停住。

core.main: standstill 强调行动、交通、经济或谈判停止推进。

usage_groups.group_title: 完全停住、没有进展
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 交通、工作、谈判或经济活动不再向前。

examples.sentence: Traffic came to a standstill after the accident.
examples.translation: 事故发生后，交通完全停住了。

examples.sentence: Negotiations reached a standstill when neither side would compromise.
examples.translation: 双方都不愿妥协，谈判陷入停滞。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象语境中指进展停止。

examples.sentence: Traffic came to a standstill after the accident.
examples.translation: 事故发生后，交通完全停住了。

examples.sentence: Negotiations reached a standstill when neither side would compromise.
examples.translation: 双方都不愿妥协，谈判陷入停滞。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象语境中指进展停止。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The bus came to a stop at the corner.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: stop 普通；standstill 强调整体进程停住。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Work came to a halt during the strike.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: halt 和 standstill 接近，halt 更像动作被叫停。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The society remained in political stasis.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: stasis 更学术，强调长期静止或无变化。

antonyms.example_sentence: The talks made progress after the compromise.
antonyms.difference_note: progress 与 standstill 的停滞相反。

extension.root_affix: standstill 来自 stand still，字面就是“站住不动”。

word_family.example_sentence: The strike brought production to a standstill.
The water was still.

related_words.example_sentence: The order brought the project to a halt.
